outcome_contract.why: Each workflow step loads skill modules that consume AI capacity. Without knowing which ones are the heaviest, it's impossible to spot waste or catch modules that are silently growing. Per-module tracking would reveal where to focus efficiency improvements and catch bloat before it drives up costs.
outcome_contract.intended_outcome_statement: Per-module context consumption is tracked in telemetry records and surfaced in reports, enabling targeted efficiency improvements.

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Report shows module files only — the stated goal
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B: focused on the stated need

engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Module-only breakdown — sufficient for the stated goal

engineering_coverage.applicable: Keys are all context paths — no explicit module identity
engineering_coverage.current_or_option_state: Keys are resolved module paths persisted at record time — stable identity
engineering_coverage.gap_or_implication: Option B: solves the module identity problem

engineering_coverage.applicable: Old records: field `undefined` — consumers must default

stage_payload.decision_summary: The workflow telemetry system aggregates context bytes across all loaded files into a single `context_input_bytes` number. This prevents identifying which modules are the heaviest consumers or detecting growth over time. The decision is what field shape to use for per-module byte tracking and how to extend the report layer without bloating the health-check consumer.
goals: Track per-module context consumption in each telemetry record
Surface per-module data in the Markdown/JSON report
Maintain backwards compatibility with old JSONL records
non_goals: Time-bucketed trend dashboards or UI visualizations
Changes to how skills pass `--module` flags
Per-file tracking for non-module context (skill SKILL.md, input paths, artifact)
constraints: Must not break existing JSONL consumers (health check, summary, report)
`computeTelemetryKey()` must include new field to prevent dedupe collisions
Old records without the new field must be handled gracefully
Module identity must be stable across time — cannot depend on filesystem state at query time
Health-check output (`WorkflowTelemetrySummary`) must not be bloated with per-module aggregates
assumptions: Module file sizes are reasonable for in-memory processing (largest module ~10KB)
The existing `readPathMetrics()` loop already reads each file — no extra I/O
`docs/business-os/startup-loop/ideas/lp-do-ideas-telemetry.schema.md` documents the persisted JSONL fields and must be updated alongside any schema change

evaluation_criteria.Why it matters: Module keys must be reliably identifiable without replaying resolution at query time
Per-module data should be easy to extract with `jq` or simple TS code
Field should match existing record patterns and the `per_module_bytes` name from the fact-find
Health check should not receive per-module aggregation overhead
Old records without the field must not break consumers

options_considered.Description: `context_path_bytes: Record<string, number>` for every context file (skill + modules + inputs + artifact)
options_considered.Downside: Cannot reliably distinguish modules from non-modules at query time without replaying `resolveModulePath()` against current filesystem — unstable over refactors; broader scope than needed; fact-find contract drift (`per_module_bytes` → `context_path_bytes`)
options_considered.Key risks: Module identity depends on filesystem state at query time

options_considered.Description: `per_module_bytes?: Record<string, number>` keyed by resolved repo-relative module paths (same paths already in `context_paths` and computed as `resolvedModules`). Empty `{}` for zero-module records; field absent (`undefined`) on legacy records — semantically distinct
options_considered.Upside: Stable module identity (resolved at record time, persisted); simple flat map query; matches fact-find contract name; focused on modules only; clean legacy/empty distinction
options_considered.Downside: Does not include non-module file sizes (not recoverable from existing records — only flat path list + aggregate total persisted)
options_considered.Key risks: Does not capture skill SKILL.md or input-path sizes — acceptable, those are less variable

options_considered.Description: Keep record unchanged; aggregate per-module only in summary by re-reading files
options_considered.Downside: Violates point-in-time measurement principle; files may have changed; not queryable from JSONL

chosen_approach.Recommendation: Option B — module-only resolved-path map `per_module_bytes: Record<string, number>` (empty `{}` when zero modules; field absent/`undefined` on legacy records)
chosen_approach.What it depends on: The `readPathMetrics()` loop already has per-file metrics and the resolved module paths. The builder needs to accumulate a `Record<string, number>` during the existing loop.
chosen_approach.Option A (all-context-paths map): Rejected because it does not solve the module identity problem. At query time, distinguishing module entries from skill/input/artifact entries in the map would require replaying `resolveModulePath()` against the current filesystem, which can resolve differently after refactors. Also introduces contract drift from the fact-find's `per_module_bytes` name.
chosen_approach.Option C (summary-time re-read): Rejected because it violates the telemetry principle of point-in-time measurement. Re-reading files at summary time would reflect current sizes, not historical.

rejected_approaches: **Option A (all-context-paths map):** Rejected because it does not solve the module identity problem. At query time, distinguishing module entries from skill/input/artifact entries in the map would require replaying `resolveModulePath()` against the current filesystem, which can resolve differently after refactors. Also introduces contract drift from the fact-find's `per_module_bytes` name.
**Option C (summary-time re-read):** Rejected because it violates the telemetry principle of point-in-time measurement. Re-reading files at summary time would reflect current sizes, not historical.

sequencing_constraints: Record type + builder + dedupe key first, then report (report depends on the record field)
risks_to_carry_forward: Dedupe key collision if `per_module_bytes` is not included in `computeTelemetryKey()` — must be part of the first task
Backwards compat — explicit handling in report code, not assumed
